name: Gainpath Execution Backlog
description: Actionable Ralph backlog for improving Gainpath across security, UX, architecture, session reliability, and AI behavior.

userStories.title: Harden auth and configuration defaults
userStories.description: Remove insecure default behavior around Basic auth, password handling, and backend configuration so the app fails safely in local and deployed environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Security-sensitive defaults in backend configuration are reviewed and tightened where practical.
Authentication flow between frontend and backend is reviewed for obvious weaknesses, especially localStorage password handling and proxy behavior.
At least one concrete hardening improvement is implemented, and any remaining risks are documented.

userStories.title: Stabilize session completion side effects
userStories.description: Review and improve the workflow that runs when a session is completed, with emphasis on background task safety, DB/session lifecycle correctness, and predictable user-visible results.
acceptanceCriteria: The completion flow in the sessions router and downstream services is reviewed end to end.
At least one concrete reliability issue in the completion pipeline is fixed.
The change preserves or improves PR detection, streak/XP updates, and post-workout coaching behavior.

userStories.title: Improve the Today flow for mobile-first usability
userStories.description: Refine the main Today experience so it feels more intentional, faster to act on, and clearer for session start, quick log, skip, and post-workout flows.
acceptanceCriteria: The Today flow receives at least one material UX improvement in the main interaction path.
The updated surface remains coherent on both mobile and desktop.

userStories.title: Audit and harden AI client fallback behavior
userStories.description: Improve the AI service layer and coaching endpoints so timeouts, invalid JSON, provider failures, and empty responses degrade in a predictable and debuggable way.
acceptanceCriteria: AI client request, retry, and fallback behavior is reviewed in code.
At least one concrete guardrail or failure-path improvement is implemented.
Usage logging remains functional for both success and failure cases.

userStories.title: Produce an architecture review with prioritized debt map
userStories.description: Create a concise architecture review covering frontend boundaries, backend layering, data flow, auth boundaries, and deployment assumptions, with practical next steps.
acceptanceCriteria: A review document is created under `.ralph-tui/reviews/US-005.md`.
The review includes severity-ranked findings tied to specific files or modules.
Recommendations distinguish short-term fixes from medium-term refactors.

userStories.title: Add focused verification for critical flows
userStories.description: Add pragmatic verification around the highest-risk backend and frontend areas touched by the earlier stories using test files, static validation, and explicit review notes. Do not block on local runtime setup or full local app testing.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one critical backend path and one critical frontend path gain meaningful verification through automated tests, focused file-level validation, or explicit review notes.
Verification targets the real flows changed by earlier stories rather than generic coverage expansion.
If local test execution or local runtime verification is unavailable, the task still completes by documenting what was verified statically and what local checks were intentionally skipped.
The result improves confidence without trying to build exhaustive test coverage or requiring a working local environment.
